item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents visibly oppose the statement from their opening turns through the end. Agent A consistently argues that individual rights should be the default and that appeals to public order or harmony can silence dissent or minorities. Agent B makes the same rights-first argument in Indonesian, emphasizing censorship, discrimination, and narrow harm-based limits. There is specific topical engagement, but little disagreement between the agents and no observable flip.
